summary: Excellent audience recording that captures a tight rhythm section and several fine moments, but the concert itself is only mildly memorable.
review: The tape delivers crystal‑clear, well‑balanced sound that rivals many matrix recordings, with drums and bass tight and no harsh wash. Musically the show offers highlights like a superb Tennessee Jed, a glowing China Doll/Playin' segment, Morning Dew and Sugar Magnolia, though the overall set is described as typical and never quite reaches brilliance.

ai_rating.rationale: Recording quality is consistently praised as excellent, while show performance is judged average‑good; the weighted combination yields a solid mid‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners note crisp, full‑range sound with no wash or boominess, tight drums and bass, nuanced capture of instruments and vocals; comparable to a matrix recording.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as typical with a few good moments; second set contains several highlights but never attains a truly magnificent performance.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Forgot the lyrics to Shakedown, but delivered a strong Tennessee Jed.
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass presence and subtle nuances are clearly heard in the recording.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocal scat on Shakedown was a crowd‑pleaser; short trip through Space noted as fun.
band_member_comments.Drums: Tight, well‑balanced, no wash or boom‑iness.
